fields.label: Status
fields.options:
Pending
Running
Paused
Stopped
Failed
Terminated

fields.description: The tenant that owns this VM, set once by Central. Empty for operator-created VMs.
fields.label: Tenant
Size preset
fields.options:
Custom
Shared 1x
Shared 2x
Shared 4x
Shared 8x
Dedicated 1x

fields.description: Guest vCPU thread count (Firecracker vcpu_count). Whole number >= 1. CPU bandwidth below or at one core is set by cpu_max_cores.
fields.label: vCPUs

fields.description: The VM's guaranteed CPU bandwidth in whole-core units. Fractional for sub-1 sizes (e.g. 0.0625 = 1/16 of a core). Defaults to vcpus (set in the controller's before_validate — a JSON default here would mask that and pin every whole-core VM to 1). In Hard cap mode this is also the hard ceiling (cgroup cpu.max); in Relaxed mode it sets the cpu.weight floor and the VM may burst above it.
fields.label: CPU bandwidth (cores)

fields.description: How cpu_max_cores is enforced. Relaxed (default): cpu_max_cores becomes a guaranteed share via cpu.weight (the floor under contention) and cpu.max is loosened to vcpus whole cores, so the VM bursts into spare host CPU when the host is idle. Hard cap: cpu_max_cores is a hard cgroup cpu.max ceiling — the VM never exceeds its share, even on an idle host.
fields.label: CPU mode

fields.description: Optional second writable disk, attached as /dev/vdb. 0 = no data disk. Set at create; grow (never shrink) via Resize.
fields.label: Data Disk (GB)

fields.description: Format the data disk ext4 and mount it at the mount point. Uncheck to attach a raw, unformatted/unmounted block device. Takes effect when the disk is first created.
fields.label: Format & Mount Data Disk

fields.description: Where the data disk is mounted inside the guest (via /etc/fstab LABEL=atlas-data).
fields.label: Data Disk Mount Point

fields.description: Internal: on-host snapshot rootfs to seed this VM's disk from (clone). Empty for a normal image-backed VM.
fields.label: Clone Source Rootfs

fields.description: Internal: on-host data-disk snapshot to seed this VM's data disk from (clone). Empty for a normal VM.
fields.label: Clone Source Data Rootfs

fields.description: Internal: the Warm snapshot this VM restores from (memory pair + MMDS identity staged at provision). Empty for every ordinary VM.
fields.label: Warm Snapshot

fields.description: Internal: the bench bake mode (site or admin) of the snapshot this VM clones from. Threaded snapshot → clone → first-boot deploy so the FQDN maps to the baked site (site) or the admin console (admin). Empty for an ordinary image-backed VM, treated as site.
fields.label: Build Mode

fields.description: Internal (routing dedup): the Site/Pilot subdomain label(s) this VM should be routed at, as a JSON list. The provisioner records them here instead of creating a Subdomain; the Satellite orchestrator reads them off the read API and reconciles its own Subdomain rows. Empty for a VM with no routed site.
fields.label: Routing Subdomains

fields.description: IPv6 is the only stable identifier outside the desk.
fields.label: SSH command

fields.description: When set, stop() refuses to stop this VM. Uncheck and save before stopping.
fields.label: Stop Protection

fields.description: When set, terminate() refuses to terminate this VM. Uncheck and save before terminating.
fields.label: Termination Protection

fields.description: Opt in: capture the VM's full memory state on stop() so the next Start resumes it in milliseconds instead of cold-booting. Restart then power-cycles back to the saved state rather than rebooting the guest. Off by default — the plain stop and cold boot remain the default path.
fields.label: Memory Snapshot on Stop

fields.description: The attached public IPv4 (a Reserved IP from this VM's Server). Empty until one is attached. Denormalized from the Reserved IP row whose virtual_machine points here.
fields.label: Public IPv4

fields.description: When set (the default, preserving today's behavior) this VM gets a public IPv6 /128, proxy-NDP, and the public forward rule. When cleared, the VM is dark: no public /128 at all, reachable only over the WireGuard host mesh via its private_address. Flipping this is rebuild-only. See llm/references/private-networking-host-mesh.md section 6.
fields.label: Public Networking

fields.description: Give the guest outbound IPv4 via host NAT44 (apt/pip/security updates). Independent of public_networking: a dark VM still gets v4 egress by default; clear this for a truly air-gapped VM reachable only inside its tenant /48 + the proxy.
fields.label: IPv4 Egress (NAT44)

fields.description: The VM's private-plane /128 on the WireGuard host mesh, derived (not allocated) from (tenant, VM UUID) so it survives migration byte-for-byte. Empty when the VM has no tenant (operator-created). Denormalized for legibility and the Subdomain denorm; the source of truth is atlas.networking.derive_private_address.
fields.label: Private Address

fields.description: Set after a keep-address migration: the source Server whose host still receives this VM's /128 and forwards it here over a per-VM tunnel (spec/19 §2.9). While set, the VM depends on that host being up; the operator can collapse the forward (falling back to a new address) from the VM form. Empty for a VM that has never been migrated keep-address, or after the forward is collapsed.
fields.label: Traffic Forwarded From

fields.description: When the keep-address forward from traffic_forwarded_from was established.
fields.label: Traffic Forwarded Since

fields.description: Mark this VM as a reverse-proxy node. Proxy VMs front the region's subdomains (the Subdomain doctype) and are reconciled by the proxy control plane. An ordinary operator-owned VM with a proxy image and an attached public IPv4.
fields.label: Is Proxy

fields.description: Mark this VM as the region's customer gateway (spec/25 Phase 5, spec/26). Customers dial its fixed reserved public IPv4 :51820 over WireGuard and land inside their tenant's /48, reaching every VM in their VPC. Carries one shared wg0 with the static same_48 eBPF guard. Reconciled by atlas.customer_gateway. Mutually exclusive with is_proxy.
fields.label: Is Gateway

fields.description: The last stop captured a complete memory snapshot; the next Start resumes from it. Cleared when the snapshot is consumed or invalidated (start, rebuild, resize, host-key rotation).
fields.label: Has Memory Snapshot
Pilot Credential ID
fields.description: Central-minted per-pilot credential id, copied from the owning Site. Echoed to Central on vm.* events so Central links/revokes the credential.
